name: golang-github-google-martian-dev
title: golang-github-google-martian-dev
summary: Martian is a library for building custom HTTP/S proxies
description:
Martian is a library for building custom HTTP/S proxies
Martian Proxy is a programmable HTTP proxy designed to be used for testing.
.
Martian is a great tool to use if you want to:
* Verify that all (or some subset) of requests are secure
* Mock external services at the network layer
* Inject headers, modify cookies or perform other mutations of HTTP requests
and responses
* Verify that pingbacks happen when you think they should
* Unwrap encrypted traffic (requires install of CA certificate in browser)
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-google-martian-dev because Martian is a library for building custom HTTP/S proxies.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-google-martian-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-google-martian-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-google-martian-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
